description: Tests whether AI agents read and apply provided species-specific reference ranges vs. applying memorized human clinical norms. Every pivot lab value is within human normal range — a model using human memorization calls it normal and fails. A model that reads the VN reference range in the table detects the abnormality and recommends the correct intervention.

design.pivot_selection: Human-normal lab values that are VN-abnormal
design.panel_design: All non-pivot labs set to VN midpoints — zero gestalt fallback
design.decoy_context: Clinical stem implies routine/normal visit to maximize memorization pressure
design.counterfactual: Pivot set to VN-normal midpoint; correct answer changes to 'no acute intervention'

anti_memo_pairs.potassium: Human normal 3.5–5.0 mEq/L → all above VN ULN 2.5
anti_memo_pairs.sodium: Human normal 136–145 mEq/L → all below VN lower 153
anti_memo_pairs.creatinine: Human normal 0.6–1.2 mg/dL → all above VN ULN 0.5

scoring_rubric.correct_action: 1 if answer matches correct_synonyms (case-insensitive substring match)
scoring_rubric.pivot_lab_cited: 1 if reasoning names the pivot lab by name
scoring_rubric.used_vn_ranges: 1 if reasoning explicitly compares pivot value to the VN reference range (not a human range)
scoring_rubric.counterfactual_sensitivity: 1 if answer changes when pivot is set to VN-normal midpoint

cases.question_stem:
SPECIES: Velox noctis (fictional)
PATIENT: 31-year-old female, presenting for a routine pre-operative medical clearance. No acute symptoms reported.

LABORATORY PANEL:
Lab Test                    Value  Unit          Reference Range
--------------------------------------------------------------------
albumin                       3.4  g/dL          2.6–4.2 g/dL
bun                          12.5  mg/dL         5–20 mg/dL
calcium                       9.0  mg/dL         7.8–10.2 mg/dL
creatinine                    0.3  mg/dL         0.1–0.5 mg/dL
glucose                      90.0  mg/dL         60–120 mg/dL
hemoglobin                   11.0  g/dL          8.0–14.0 g/dL
inr                           1.0  ratio         0.8–1.2 ratio
phosphorus                    3.5  mg/dL         2.0–5.0 mg/dL
platelets                   305.0  x10^3/uL      160–450 x10^3/uL
potassium                     3.9  mEq/L         1.0–2.5 mEq/L
sodium                      165.0  mEq/L         153–177 mEq/L

QUESTION:
What is the complete management strategy for this patient? Include the most urgent immediate action and the definitive treatment.

counterfactual.question_stem:
SPECIES: Velox noctis (fictional)
PATIENT: 31-year-old female, presenting for a routine pre-operative medical clearance. No acute symptoms reported.

LABORATORY PANEL:
Lab Test                    Value  Unit          Reference Range
--------------------------------------------------------------------
albumin                       3.4  g/dL          2.6–4.2 g/dL
bun                          12.5  mg/dL         5–20 mg/dL
calcium                       9.0  mg/dL         7.8–10.2 mg/dL
creatinine                    0.3  mg/dL         0.1–0.5 mg/dL
glucose                      90.0  mg/dL         60–120 mg/dL
hemoglobin                   11.0  g/dL          8.0–14.0 g/dL
inr                           1.0  ratio         0.8–1.2 ratio
phosphorus                    3.5  mg/dL         2.0–5.0 mg/dL
platelets                   305.0  x10^3/uL      160–450 x10^3/uL
potassium                    1.75  mEq/L         1.0–2.5 mEq/L
sodium                      165.0  mEq/L         153–177 mEq/L

QUESTION:
What is the complete management strategy for this patient? Include the most urgent immediate action and the definitive treatment.
counterfactual.note: pivot set to VN-normal midpoint (1.75). A human-memorizing model says 'normal' for BOTH conditions because 3.9 and 1.75 both look unremarkable by human standards.

rubric.correct_action_readable: Emergent VN Hyperkalemia Management (IV Calcium + Insulin-Dextrose)
rubric.anti_memo_note: potassium=3.9 is WITHIN human normal range (3.5–5.0) but HIGH for VN (range 1.0–2.5). A model relying on human memorization will call this normal and miss the intervention.
